Analyze error & uncertainty

The basics of error propagation, plus power-analysis and uncertainty tooling.

There is error in everything — in measurement tools, in averages, in propagation. The methods you apply depend on what you’re doing. The following covers the basics.

Power analysis

A helpful tool for initial power analyses and sample-size calculations: sample-size calculator.

Propagation of errors (for standard deviations)

If you have errors and are doing arithmetic with the values, you must propagate the errors. Let Δx and Δy be the errors on measured variables x and y.

Addition / subtraction — for z = x ± y:

Δz = sqrt( (Δx)² + (Δy)² )

Multiplication / division — for z = x·y or z = x/y:

Δz / |z| = sqrt( (Δx/x)² + (Δy/y)² )

Power — for z = xⁿ:

Δz / |z| = |n| · (Δx / |x|)

Measured quantity times a constant — for z = A·x:

Δz = |A| · Δx

Resources

  • R: Dolph Schluter’s guide to statistics in R
  • Python: the uncertainties library
  • MATLAB: movmean and related moving-statistics tools
